stories.title: The U.S. blockade continues despite Iran's announcement the Strait of Hormuz is open
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.

stories.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
stories.description: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

stories.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

stories.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

stories.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
stories.description: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

stories.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
stories.description: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Since the start of the US blockade on Monday, 15 vessels have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, nine of which have links to Iran, BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests.

stories.title: Where is US-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin?
stories.description: This week, FRANCE 24's media show Scoop looks at growing international alarm after US-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin was detained in Kuwait. Prior to his arrest, Shihab-Eldin shared publicly available videos and images related to the Iran war. Press and human rights groups...

stories.title: Live: Trump welcomes Iran's announcement that Strait of Hormuz is fully open to shipping
Starmer, Macron push forward efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday chair a meeting of allies to consider sending a multinational force to ensure security and free-flowing trade in the Strait of Hormuz once the current conflict between Iran and the US and Israel ends. F...

stories.title: Britain, France chair meeting on Hormuz
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. The force would help ensure...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Macron, Starmer meet at Elysée Palace
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. The force would help ensure...

stories.title: Lebanon erupts in joy as 10-day ceasefire with Israel takes effect
stories.description: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran, the US and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

stories.title: 'Older women are in demand by younger men': Papers discuss dating trends
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, April 17: We take a look at how papers are reacting to the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon. Next: the UK suffers food shortages "from Trump's war". Also: did Pete Hegseth really quote from "Pulp Fiction" in his latest war prayer? Finally, we take a loo...

stories.title: Europe leads postwar security talks
stories.description: Donald Trump has said that another round of talks between the United States and Iran could take place as soon as this weekend. Meanwhile, this Friday, the French and British leaders are due to chair a meeting of around 40 countries focused on securing the Strait of Hormuz. France...

stories.title: Iran foreign minister says Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’
stories.description: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.title: The three clocks of the Iran war
stories.description: Trump is racing against the midterms, Iran is betting on endurance, and Netanyahu needs a war with no end.

stories.title: How the Lebanon ceasefire is viewed in Israel
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Tony Cheng describes how people and the press in Israel are reacting to the ceasefire with Lebanon.

stories.title: The Iran war has exposed the limits of neutrality
stories.description: From Gulf energy shocks to divisions among allies, the conflict has made strategic ambiguity harder to sustain.

stories.title: Could the Lebanon ceasefire lead to US-Iran talks breakthrough?
stories.description: Al Jazeera's Tohid Asadi discusses the prospect that the ceasefire in Lebanon could lead to a US-Iran peace deal.

stories.title: Lebanese celebrate return to homes as 10-day ceasefire begins
stories.description: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has come into effect.

stories.title: Lebanese return to devastated south as fragile 10-day truce takes hold
stories.description: Hezbollah warns it has its 'finger on the trigger' in case of Israeli violations of the temporary ceasefire.

stories.title: Australia scrambles to secure energy as war on Iran fuels uncertainty
stories.description: Heavily dependent on imported oil, Australia looks for quick fixes from regional diplomacy to free train rides.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz open for commercial vessels after Lebanon deal
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz, vital for the global trade in oil and other commodities, was open following a ceasefire accord agreed in Lebanon. Araqchi said in a post on X that the Strait was open for all commercial vessels for t...

stories.title: Pakistan escorted Iranian negotiators home from peace talks amid concerns of Israeli attack, sources say
stories.description: The air force escorted Iranian negotiators home from inconclusive peace talks with the United States last weekend, launching a major operation after the Iranians said Israel might seek to kill them, three sources told Reuters. Pakistan deployed some two dozen jets in the escort, ...

stories.title: Trump hints at Islamabad trip if Iran deal is finalised
stories.description: PRIME Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim shake hands in Doha; and (right) Field Marshal Asim Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf in Tehran.—Reuters/AFP • Claims Iran has accepted ‘almost everything’ in talks• Nuclear issue remains ke...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz to meet President Erdogan, attend diplomacy forum on third leg of tour today
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif will meet Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and attend the Antalya Diplomacy Forum today on the third leg of a tri-nation tour, the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) said on Friday. In a statement on X, the PMO said that the prime minister will part...

stories.title: Russian strikes kill 19 across Ukraine
stories.description: KYIV: A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 19 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with talks on ending the conflict stalled. Journalists heard loud bangs echoing over the capital during the night and saw huge plumes of black s...

stories.title: Israeli army fire kills Palestinian teenager in West Bank
stories.description: RAMALLAH: Israeli forces killed a 17-year-old Palestinian in the occupied West Bank on Thursday, according to Palestinian officials. Mohammed Murad Mahmud Rayan was “killed by the (Israeli) occupation forces on Thursday morning in Beit Duqqu”, a village just north of Jerusalem, t...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran announces reopening of Strait of Hormuz for remaining period of ceasefire
stories.description: Trump follows up with his own announcement on the Strait’s opening, but says naval blockade ‌on ‌Iran ⁠will “remain ⁠in full force” until ​a ‌deal is struck; crude prices tumble 10%

stories.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ for commercial vessels till remaining period of ceasefire
stories.description: While welcoming Iran’s decision to reopen the Strait, Trump says the naval blockade against Iran will remain in force till a deal is reached.

stories.title: First loaded Iranian oil tankers exit Gulf since U.S. blockade: Kpler
stories.description: The Deep Sea, Sonia I and Diona, all under U.S. sanctions, passed the strategic strait on April 15, 2026 after leaving Iran’s Kharg Island, having loaded on April 2, 8 and 9 respectively

stories.title: Israel says military operation against Hezbollah ‘still not complete’
stories.description: Israel Defence Minister Israel Katz also warned that if the fighting resumed, displaced residents returning to the country’s war-torn south would have to evacuate again

stories.title: Trump says it is important for Pope to understand Iran is a global threat
stories.description: "The Pope has to understand — it's ​very simple — Iran cannot ‌have a nuclear weapon. The world would be in great danger," says Mr. Trump

stories.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with first American pontiff
stories.description: Pope Leo, who is known for choosing his words carefully, ‌has emerged as an outspoken ​critic of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran that ⁠began on February 28
